reasoning: The informal statement asserts infinitely many primes p satisfying p ≡ -1 (mod 4). The Lean statement uses the standard "for every N there exists p ≥ N with property P" encoding of infinitude, which is logically equivalent to "infinitely many p with property P". The condition `p + 1 ≡ 0 [MOD 4]` unfolds (via `Nat.ModEq`, defined as `(p+1) % 4 = 0 % 4 = 0`) to `4 ∣ (p+1)`, i.e., `p ≡ 3 ≡ -1 (mod 4)`, which exactly captures "congruent to -1 modulo 4". `Nat.Prime p` correctly expresses primality. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the quantifier structure faithfully expresses infinitude.
